subsystem.function: Base plate with mounting holes, leveling, and cable entry

instruction: Design only this subsystem: P0 plan, part_tree children, 12-24 coordinate-bearing geometry_ops. Every evidence_feature must appear. Do not modify other subsystems or the global envelope.

criteria.good: Parts are integrated only when service access, inspection, and interface clarity remain visible.
criteria.bad: Monolithic body hides removable modules, datums, or inspection paths.
criteria.loop_feedback: Consolidate only low-risk covers or labels; keep service modules, datums, and inspection windows visible.

criteria.good: Fastener patterns, pin joints, slots, bosses, and mating clearances are explicitly represented.
criteria.bad: Joints are described in prose but not represented as holes, bosses, pins, slots, or datums.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add visible fastener holes, bosses, joint pins, and clearance notes for each critical interface.

criteria.good: A reviewer can see what to inspect and why without opening raw JSON.
criteria.bad: Design has no callouts, access windows, labels, datum pads, or verification focus.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add inspection callouts, datum labels, visible windows, and verification checks tied to geometry features.

criteria.good: Critical joints state locking method (locknut, insert, washer, adhesive note) and torque/preload intent; bolt patterns match load direction.
criteria.bad: Fasteners appear as bare holes with no locking, washer, engagement-length, or preload language anywhere.
criteria.loop_feedback: For each critical joint, name the locking feature, add washer/insert geometry, and state a torque or preload intent note.

criteria.good: Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole.
criteria.bad: Screws thread directly into thin printed walls or blind features with no boss, insert, or depth note.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add insert bosses with engagement depth >= 1.5x screw diameter and surrounding wall thickness for every threaded joint.

criteria.good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
criteria.bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.
criteria.loop_feedback: Separate the critical service module and add a visible removal path, latch, pin, or slide rail.

criteria.good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
criteria.bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add external or inspectable harness channels with strain relief and access covers.

criteria.good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.
criteria.bad: Access is claimed only in prose.
criteria.loop_feedback: Convert service-access text into physical geometry_ops: cover, pocket, slot, window, and fastener pattern.

criteria.good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
criteria.bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add strain-relief clamps or grommets at every cable exit, state a minimum bend radius, and reroute harnesses away from hinge lines and sharp edges.

criteria.good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
criteria.bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.
criteria.loop_feedback: Reorient connectors toward access openings, add finger clearance pockets, and engrave keying/ID labels next to each connector.

criteria.good: P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
criteria.bad: P0 lists features but geometry_ops are generic boxes at the origin.

criteria.good: Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume.
criteria.bad: Geometry is stacked at [0,0,0] or lacks coordinates.

criteria.good: Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer — not just boxes.
criteria.bad: Only box and cylinder ops with no subtractive or finishing features.

criteria.good: Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops (body+subtract+interface+finish).
criteria.bad: Some children have 0-1 ops (LOW-RES). All detail concentrated on primary body.

criteria.good: A primary datum feature (flat, bore, or pin) is explicitly named and placed.
criteria.bad: No datum reference — assembly alignment is uncontrolled.

criteria.good: Adjacent interfaces include fit_class (H7/h6, LC2) and stack_up_mm.
criteria.bad: Interfaces have no tolerance or fit specification.

criteria.good: cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
criteria.bad: No assembly order — build sequence is ambiguous.

criteria.good: Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, drill, subtract, pocket. Subtractive features (drill/pocket/channel) and finishing (fillet/chamfer) both present.
criteria.bad: Only basic box/cylinder ops. Missing subtractive or finishing features typical for this seed.

criteria.good: Dimensions match robot_arm scale: 80-400mm per link, 600-1200mm reach. Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible.
criteria.bad: Dimensions are placeholder (all 10mm) or unrealistic for the subsystem scale.

citable_sources.title: NASA Systems Engineering Handbook SP-2016-6105 Rev2
1800 Mechanical Movements, Devices and Appliances (Hiscox, 1899, public domain)

boundary_note: Subsystem scale: 80-400mm per link, 600-1200mm reach. Environment: industrial floor, IP54, 10-40C ambient. Design loads: payload 2-10kg at tip, joint torque 5-50Nm, 10k+ cycles. Structural: include fixture_points with [x,y,z] and type (fixed/pinned) in verify.boundary_conditions.

operability_profile.operating_principle: Serial manipulator architecture that carries torque through joints/links, routes cables along the spine, and exposes serviceable actuator/sensor modules.
